hypothesis: If amorphous materials are generated using the AMDEN EGNN-based diffusion model with VE/VP noise schedules and energy-based HMC-refined denoising, then the resulting structures will match MD reference RDFs, bond-angle and structure-factor statistics and potential-energy distributions and can be conditioned to achieve a target Young’s modulus, because energy-informed scoring and in-sampling equilibration bias denoising toward physically consistent configurations.
